notes: Zero ambiguous findings — rule is crisp on NOT NULL without DEFAULT.
Precision measured at 100% on 19 findings, well above the 80% blocking threshold.

Pre-registration gap: bar committed in 2b5d1fb at 17:20 on the same day, after the classification work was recorded in dad8a42 at 09:29. DM-18 is calibrated under a retroactively formalized bar. Gap acknowledged in 51d767d on 2026-04-13.
Evidence JSONL exists locally at scripts/mvp-migration/reports/calibration-postfix-2026-04-12.jsonl but is not currently committed to the public repo. Decision about whether to commit it is deferred.
First shape measured under a fully pre-registered bar. Bar was committed seven minutes before measurement.

Produced 0 findings on the full 761-migration corpus. 39 DROP COLUMN occurrences in the corpus, none with live FK dependents at drop time because Prisma emits DROP CONSTRAINT before DROP COLUMN in its defensive migration sequence.
This is the first held-to-bar negative result in verify's history and the first demonstration that the discipline rejects attempts under pressure.
Zero findings is below the 10-finding sample-size floor, so no precision is computed and no promotion is considered. The rule stays in shipped, warning-only in CI.
Second held-to-bar negative result, measured under the same pre-registered bar as DM-15.
Produced 0 findings on the full 761-migration corpus. 15 DROP TABLE occurrences in the corpus, none with live FK dependents at drop time for the same defensive-sequence reason as DM-15.

Two consecutive zero-result attempts on shapes from the same family (FK-drop safety) establish a pattern about the corpus, not about the rules. The 761-migration corpus is structurally unable to fire the safety-shape family because Prisma's generator pre-empts the failure modes.
